steps.title: Find the constant ratio
steps.content:
Since $k$ to $p$ is constant, the ratio $\frac{k}{p}$ remains the same for all pairs of values. Using $k = 9$ and $p = 8$:
$$\frac{k}{p} = \frac{9}{8}$$

steps.title: Apply the ratio to find k when p = 504
steps.content:
Set up the proportion with the constant ratio and the new value of $p$:
$$\frac{k}{504} = \frac{9}{8}$$
Multiply both sides by 504:
$$k = 504 \cdot \frac{9}{8} = 63 \cdot 9 = 567$$
That matches Choice D — 567.
